chunks.text: Space development was once an undertaking carried only by nations.
But in the twenty - first century, private companies have entered one after another
and the " commercial space age " has arrived.
A leading company is Elon Musk's SpaceX.
It developed reusable rockets and dramatically reduced launch costs.
It achieved, in the private sector
technology at the same level as a formal state program.
Mars colonization too, rather than a lord's fantasy, has entered a concrete stage.
A giant rocket called Starship aims for a crewed Mars flight in the 2030 s.
But practically, the challenges pile up.
The one - way trip to Mars is six to nine months.
Cosmic radiation, osteoporosis, psychological isolation.
To cure these problems requires many years of research.
Even when one arrives on Mars, the environment is unsuited to life.
The atmosphere's constitute is mainly carbon dioxide, and temperatures are below zero.
Water freezes, and plants do not grow.
Even so, humanity keeps taking up the challenge.
Why?
There is no single answer, but the scientist Carl Sagan said
" Earth is only a small dot.
By living on multiple planets, humanity increases its confidence of survival.
There are also those opposed to Mars colonization.
" It would only pollute a new planet without solving Earth's problems.
" " The vast funds should be used to end poverty.
" There is legitimate basis for this criticism.
Space development expands the possibilities of humanity.
But at the same time
we must not forget the responsibility to care for the Earth.
When we look up at the sky
we must direct our eyes to both the future and the present.
